text.content:
Mission Setup Time: 20-30 Minutes.

Now that the Afterlife's been opened to the public, there are two key features that the owner wants to prioritize. The second updating the hotel's caliber of service and materials, and that will require a crafty and experienced Head Of House.

This mission unlocks the Hotel's services on completion, if Rodger Stout is rescued successfully. If he isn't, the Afterlife can hire someone else to improve the services at GM discretion.

The Candle Flickers

Friend Opportunity: Mr. Stout

Mr. Rodger Stout is a distinguished English gentleman and an efficient operator, having spent his life running the lives of those who hired him to do so. If befriended, he can be relied upon to provide his coordination skills to your followers. He takes well to those who can follow directions while still offering input, and dislikes rebels or mindless drones.

Enemy Opportunity - Retirement Home Owner

The Shady Shallows Retirement Home is located in the University District, and lives up to its name. Its proprietor is a known swindler with all kinds of nasty connections. Can be replaced by an enemy with a gang-aligned, or corp-aligned, background.

Rep Opportunity: The Sleepers Must Awaken

The first character to rouse the sleepers to action earns this achievement.

Gig Payout

300eb + 2 Eagles per player, with a bonus of 100eb + 1 Eagle per player if Mr. Stout is recovered without injury and gives the go-ahead.

Beat Chart

Beat 1: Meeting Sammy & His Dad

Once again meeting with Sammy, they're introduced to his only surviving family and given the assignment - the Afterlife needs a head of house, and the old man knows exactly who'd be a perfect fit.

Beat 2: Where's Rodger's Neighborhood?

The players are asked to locate Rodger, by speaking with one of his former employers.

Beat 3: Rescuing Rodger

Having acquired everything they need, the party makes their way to the Shady Shallows Retirement Home and discovers a sad truth, and reveals a monster wearing human skin.

Beat 4: Finding His Faculties

The party needs to recover some chipsoft from Mr. Stout's former employer, and are faced with a choice when doing so.

Beat 5: Returning Rodger

Now possessed of his full faculties once again, Rodger can be returned to the Afterlife, where he begins work immediately. And, most interestingly, he insists on meeting the owner - an event that Sammy says is long overdue for the players, as well…

The FM Receiver Crackles, The Pirate Wavelength Carrying A Cool, Seductive Female Voice…

"Good evening Night Citizens, this is Nightlight, voice of the city, here to take the scream out of the sheets and into your ears as a cool whisper, fingers down the back of your neck, and eyes in the back of your head. You can never have too many pairs unless you're Maelstrom and you take that idea literally, so keep 'em open out there, NC.

"Tonight's top story: Night Corp has released their latest report on NC's age demographics, and to no one's surprise it's still skewed sharper than a Piranha's personality after a Blue Glass binge. All you beautiful 20-somethings out there running the gangs, enjoy it while it lasts, 'cause statistically you still aren't likely to make it past 55. You take out the old folks who live in their ivory towers in Little Europe and the Glen, and we may as well be partying like it's 1555, 'cept this time it ain't infant mortality rates that are bringing down the curve. Turns out those old bones from the 20-teens and 20s just can't keep up with the new blood where it counts, speedware or not, but maybe you'll be the exception to the rule.

"But if you are looking to party, I've got it on good authority that the rumored cloud-city in Upper Marina has finally opened its doors, and if you're in the know, I wouldn't wait to get down there. The party's raging every night below Night City's streets, and puts a new spin on the term 'ground floor opportunity.'

"Last but not least, it looks like our 6th Street Saviors only bought us a temporary reprieve. Now that they've been flushed out of South Night City, we're getting reports of scav abductions from Westbrook all the way to Pacifica, and everywhere in between. Keep your eyes on those side alleys, Night Owls, and if you're able, maybe try a little pest control of your own. It takes a village to raise a child and it takes a city to maintain one - street vermin are everyone's problem.

"Up next we've got some electroswing for you to pop heads and swing your swords to - last century classics with a funky fresh twist! Until next time NC, this has been Nightlight, signing off and hoping I made your night a little brighter.

This mission makes use of the @UUID[Scene.fqgvewCqt8z94mll]{7th Circle Bar & Casino} and @UUID[Scene.kCCmgpQ8ydS06ngn]{Shady Shallows Retirement Home} scenes, along with a few environments. To begin:

  • Populate the Afterlife with guests at your preference - civilians, gangsters, corpos, whatever.

  • Place @UUID[Actor.Y7NnvbMut6swzVaA]{Checkers} at one of the tables in the gambling pit.

  • Place @UUID[Actor.ulLREe1e9ZU3s2bv]{Metrovsky Petrovitch} at the table Silvers is sitting at, as well as 1-3 other tokens of your choosing. Leave enough space at the table for 2 other people to join.

    • A small crowd has also gathered around the table - it's the evening's high-roller game.

  • Place @UUID[Actor.rQdrdTOYV13yilZD]{Sammy} behind the bar and @UUID[Actor.PJBDw6Qy61QZGIXp]{Silvers} at the door.

  • Place @UUID[Actor.8UOCtWf5IJ7wa8y7]{David Valdez} in front of the bar.

  • Place the player tokens inside the scene.

  • Ask players for entry payment - the Afterlife will comp anyone there on business, but any comped players won't get the benefits of the Afterlife's services.

  • Change the scene to @UUID[Scene.VCbthP8Ez4uhtdkr]{Afterlife Exterior Environment.}

Then read the following:

Burning Wax

Following your meeting with the Afterlife's Owner, Sammy is true to Vido's word, and reaches out to each of you personally soon afterwards - and for once, he's nice and up-front with the details.

Evidently it's time for the Afterlife to put it's upper floors to use, and to do that, the club needs a head of house - someone to make sure the sheets stay clean and the hallways pristine.

Sammy's got an idea, but it's not his own - his old man knew a guy who knew a guy who'd be perfect for the job, and Papa Chauvin just so happens to owe the mystery man a favor. The trouble is, nobody knows where the future master housekeeper actually is - somewhere in Night City for sure, but that's a wide net to cast.

He's asked you all to the Afterlife this evening, to talk to his pops and see if you can track down where this other old fella might be. Somewhat of an odd job to be sure, but not the strangest you've seen.

Inside the 7th Circle

Inside the Afterlife, it's already business as usual, even though the club's only been open a few days. Clarisa is dancing wildly on the floor as if nothing had ever happened to her and, fortunately, doesn't notice you come in. The gambling pit is full, tonight, thanks to a local tournament, and it's the first time you've seen so many gangbangers of different stripes coexisting so peacefully in one place.

At the bar, Sammy's busy slinging booze to anyone who asks. Sitting across from him is a silver fox that can only be his father, with a beautiful young woman on either side of him. Despite his age, he looks every bit as good as Sammy does, and without a trace of visible cyberware, either. Either he won the genetic lottery, or his cosmetic surgeon is a few cuts above what you'd otherwise expect.

It's obvious one takes after the other, though it's unclear who's which. Sammy Sr. has the same waxed mustache, the same troublemaking smile, and the same inexplicable taste for banana-yellow suits.

He's most remarkable for being one of the few old people you've run into in Night City thus far. The Time of the Red hasn't been kind to senior citizens, and most of those who've survived are execs up in their towers, or else in retirement pods squirreled away in basements and apartments.

Sammy points and grins as you approach, and his father stands up off his barstool to offer his hand to the group. The two women stand up as well, and head off to a table nearby to wait for him. "David Valdez, otherwise known as Mr. Chauvin Senior around these parts. A pleasure to meet you."

Sammy's father is cordial and impeccably polite, even if his conversation partners… aren't. See his character sheet for notes on how to roleplay him.

He and Sammy have a good relationship built on mutual respect, forged atop a rocky history, uprooted by the death of Sammy's mother and sister in Guatemala and their differing responses to it; Sammy left and became an edgerunner in LA, while David pursued and ultimately found revenge for his family.

Regardless of this storied history, though, David's in Night City for the long haul now, pursuing a well-earned retirement with the help of his son. When made aware of the Afterlife's staffing needs, he volunteered someone to fill the role, but needs help tracking him down. He shares the following information.

  • In his storied history, Mr. Valdez has known many executives and their assistants - gentlemen of the highest sort, though neither afraid to get their hands dirty.

  • The exemplar of this idea was one Mr. Rodger Stout, a British head of household whom David knew throughout Stout's work for several different execs.

  • David and Rodger were good friends - though their life paths differed, they bonded over the other's commitment to excellence, virtue, and exceptionalism in their chosen paths.

  • As far as David is aware, Rodger is living in Night City, having moved there around the same time David did (1 year ago).

    • However, in the last 3 months or so, Rodger has seemingly disappeared, and that has Mr. Valdez worried.

  • David has one lead - Rodger's former employer, a high level exec at SovOil and the man in charge of their few remaining operations in Night City.

    • Lucky you - it so happens that the exec in question happens to be nearby. Quite nearby, in fact.

    • With help from the Afterlife's network, Sammy and David have sequestered the exec in the gambling pit, where he's being kept occupied by Checkers as part of tonight's high-roller table.

    • Mr. Valdez was considering joining the game himself, but at Sammy's recommendation, offers the chance to the players, instead.

      • Up to 2 chairs at the table of 5 are still open, thanks to Checkers being in on the scheme.

  • With all this said, David cautions the players - he doesn't know what happened to Rodger, and execs are dangerous. Given the circumstances, it's unlikely that Rodger's whereabouts are mere happenstance.

Mr. Valdez will also provide the players with a general physical description of Mr. Stout, and point out the exec in question, before wishing them luck.

This beat uses the gambling rules that were utilized in Checkered Past, which can be found in the Services To The Afterlife journal entry 4.

The beat begins when players sit down at Checkers' table in the Afterlife, to play a game with Petrovitch. Up to 2 players can join the game.

To begin, read the following:

A High-Stakes Game Has Amassed Quite A Crowd

"About time," Silvers says as you approach. She gestures to the empty chairs. "Been waiting for you. Now that we're here, we can get started. Tonight's starting ante is 8 Eagles, or equivalent in cash." (Note: Eagles are worth 100eb each)

If the party combined doesn't have the ante for a given round, the Afterlife will compensate them for any losses, but neither will they keep any winnings, should they succeed at the game.

This is a 9 round high-roller table, due largely to the ante increasing as the game goes on (at rounds 4 and 7, specifically).

If the players lose the game at any point, refer to the final section of this journal entry.

The primary way this scene progresses is with Metrovsky sharing information by round, as follows.

Night City Holdem - Play-By-Play

Round 1 (Ante - 8 Eagles):

  • Metrovsky takes an interest in the players who sat down immediately, asking after their interesting features (unusual cyberware, high rep score, etc.) in small talk.

Round 2:

  • Metrovsky wonders at where the players acquired their wealth, and suggests that it might be good for business.

Round 3:

  • Metrovsky will share a little about himself - his SovOil standing, his status as head of Night City operations, and express regret that the Afterlife's current bouncer so heavily kneecapped his predecessors.

Round 4 (Ante - Raised to 11 Eagles):

  • Metrovsky receives a call via one of his team members and tells them to handle it, then apologizes to the game. He's looking for better hired help.

Round 5:

  • If another exec is nearby, Metrovsky commiserates with them over the lack of good help, and shares that he used to know someone who filled the role.

Round 6:

  • Metrovsky realizes he's loosing focus on the game, From this point on, any player can attempt a DV17 conversation check as their round action to make a bet with Metrovsky - in addition to the table ante, they can bet their services for the location of Rodger.

    • The check can be reattempted once each round; the DV increases by 2 for each failure.

    • If the players lose, Petrovitch sends them on a suicide mission befitting an edgerunner, at GM discretion, likely against Petrochem.

    • In order to win, the player actively betting must play 3 rounds/9 checks directly against Petrovitch (rolling off in place of a set DV) and win more of them than Petrovitch.

      • This is done separately from determining who wins the table ante in the main game, and can happen regardless of the outcome of the game.

    • The mission takes place in outer New Westbrook, just outside the Night City limits, and involves the party sabotaging a Petrochem system which Petrochem has contracted Militech to guard.

    • Players can choose to simply not do the mission, but doing so makes Petrovitch a party enemy.

Round 7 (Ante - raised to 13 Eagles):

  • In lieu of further conversation around his servantry, Metrovsky asks the players where they learned to gamble.

Round 8:

  • Metrovsky shares that he went to university in Moscow, where everything from grades to job opportunities to sexual favors could be settled at a card table.

Round 9:

  • Metrovsky is silent; focusing on the final outcome of the game.

If the players beat all 9 rounds without making a bet to learn Rodger's location, Petrovitch is impressed with them, and calls up Vido (or works directly via a reputable fixer or face in the party) to make them a job offer to raze the retirement home and kill his former servant, counting on the Afterlife's guarantees of contract enforcement to ensure the job gets done.

  • The pay for killing Rodger/destroying the home is 5 Eagles to each party member, received on proof of job completion.

What Happened To Rodger?

  • Rodger Stout worked for Metrovsky for several years, including during his migration to Night City, but discovered some unfortunate evidence of corruption that could seriously harm Petrovitch's standing within his corporation.

    • The information, which will only ever be revealed with a DV21 Interrogation check against Petrovitch, is that the exec was trading his corporate knowledge with a Petrochem partner and the two have been insider trading as a result.

  • Rather than risk Rodger's loyalty, Metrovsky arranged for his assistant to received some prototype neuro-soft designed to compensate for aged and failing mental faculties.

  • Once the chipware was implanted, an "unfortunate accident" occurred, the chip effectively producing an Alzheimer's-like state within Rodger, while retaining Metrovsky's plausible deniability in his sudden disappearance.

    • Stout has amassed many friends of his own over the years and a rather powerful network as well, hence Metrovsky's concern.

  • Stout's "compassionate employer" paid for Rodger to be stored in a retirement home in New Westbrook - one with a reputation for the people within it living only a few months. Problem solved.

Petrovsky will not part with any of this information easily, but the address of the retirement home/Rodger's location are the easiest parts - not even Rodger knows that he was set up by his former employer, and Metrovsky will lie easily to state that he doesn't know the details of the condition, or that the chipware can't be removed without killing Rodger.

So You Lost - Now What?

If players lost the game prior to learning anything of value or to the final showdown, they have two options: they can either buy back in, or they can pursue other means of collecting the information. These might include:

  • Stealing Petrovitch's agent and cracking it to find the location of the retirement home.

    • Requires a DV21 Pick Pocket check and a DV17 Electronics/Security or DV11 Netrunning check

    • Note that the Afterlife will punish thieves with excommunication or assassination, so do it off the Afterlife premises.

  • Locating Petrovitch's apartment in Little Europe (a penthouse suite atop a brownstone, 8th floor) and hacking his computer.

    • How the apartment location is sourced is up the the GM, but probably a task for a Fixer or Media, or else tailing Petrovitch home. Average checks for this encounter should hover around DV17.

  • Interrogating Petrovitch themselves.

    • Checks for this range from DV17 to 24 depending on the pieces of information that the players want to extract (listed in the prior section).

  • Cutting a deal with Petrovitch.

    • If the party has a reputable fixer or exec, Petrovitch will hire them to assassinate Stout and raze the retirement home; failing to do either will make him an enemy of the party (GM discretion).

  • Forgetting Petrovitch entirely and canvassing the retirement homes in Night City themselves.

    • This process will likely take a few days, with checks made at GM discretion. Start at DV24 for 1 day, and lower the DV depending on how much extra time the party chooses to take.

Once players have learned the whereabouts of Rodger, proceed to Beat 3.

This beat continues the scene from beat 3; no new enemies are placed, but be ready to bring in the scav reinforcements if appropriate - see Scav Reinforcements for more details.

Who Goes There?

Once awoken, Rodger is delirious, thanks to the spiked chipware in his head. Once the chipware is removed (any qualified doctor would spot it eventually), he'll recover naturally after a couple weeks, but this process can be expedited through inserting an anti-Alzheimer's chip. One can be found among the scrap in the chop shop.

Scav Reinforcements

Unless the party was especially stealthy, it's likely that the scavs will have notified reinforcements, either via the receptionist or from the guards hitting a panic button.

These reinforcements will arrive in the parking lot on the north side of the map, and form a perimeter before entering. If no violence has occurred, the scavs will try to figure it if/who/how many people know about their operation before attacking.

In either case, they won't usually allow the party to leave alive, and will do everything they can to return the sleeper(s) to their bed(s).

If they are intimidated or persuaded to stand down, the entire operation will vanish within 12 hours, leaving nothing but an empty building.

Awakening the Sleepers

If/when the players realize what's going on, they might want to rescue the rest of the folks in the home. Doing so requires a DV17 Electronics/Security check at any terminal in the building with admin access, or a DV11 Netrunning check from any access point with admin access.

Once awoken, the sleepers use the stats of Civllian mooks, except they have 10 current HP, their Physical skill has been reduced to 5, and their REF stat has been increased to 7, and their Martial skill has been increased to 4 (note: REF taken into consideration for Martial number, default civillians show 7: 2 in skill + 5 in REF).

They might be delirious similar to Stout, at GM discretion. They've received combat training while in stasis thanks to the battle royale they were forced to fight in, and are semi-capable fighters.

This beat can be skipped; once it's resolved, move onto beat 6.

The remainder of this mission makes use of the same setup as Beat 3.

Rescuing Rodger

The building is as broken down on the inside as it is on the outside. The floor and ceiling are both mildewing, and in dire need of repair. The plaster on the walls has yellowed and is starting to crumble. The center clearing might've once been a garden, but has been reduced to a broken patch of dirt with nothing of interest but a pair of dead trees. Security cameras keep vigil over the area.

Locating Rodger isn't too difficult; the security cameras can be hacked with a DV9 Netrunning check to spy on the rooms, and the reception/admin computers have a registry of all the guests (also a DV9 Netrunning check, or DV15 Electronics/Security). Just visiting the pods until you find him will also work.

The area is being patrolled by security staff at GM discretion.

Getting him out is another matter entirely (note: see "Once Rodger's removed" below for consequences).

  • Rodger is hooked up to a BD wreath and life support systems which require checks to remove safely.

    • He can be released from the pod via any terminal in the building, once one has been accessed. This includes the terminal at the front of his pod, which is a DV9

    • The BD wreath requires a DV15 Cybertech check to remove, or DV21 Electronics security, but this check can be skipped if he was removed via terminal.

    • The life support systems require a DV15 Paramedic check to remove, or DV21 First Aid.

    • If these checks are failed, the apparatuses can still be removed, but doing so inflicts 1d6 damage (max: 3d6 per check) to Rodger for every 2 below the DV that the check was failed by.

      • Players can see the evidence of this failure - blood from areas where IVs were removed, seizures, foaming at the mouth, etc.

Once he has been awoken, Rodger will be in a daze.

  • He will refer to female characters as "Martha," androgynous characters as "Sam" and male characters as "Thomas," attempt to issue them housekeeping orders, and will not remember who he's serving, how he got where he is, or what happened to him in the BD chamber.

    • A DV17 Paramedic or Cybertech check will reveal the cause for this addled state as a chip in his neuralware socket, which can be safely removed without issue (though doing so will immediately cause him to pass out).

      • Anyone who passes such a check also learns that, once the chip is removed, Rodger's mental faculties will slowly return to him over the course of weeks.

      • This process could be accelerated by retrieving a Miracle Chip - a bit of chipware that acts as a mental stimulant, reducing or in some cases eliminating the effects of Alzheimer's.

      • These effects are also suppressed while he's playing Acheron (the battle royale that the scavs were forcing the sleepers to play) as the BD has been designed to overcome such problems.

Once he's removed - Rodger was at the top of the leaderboard in the current dethmatch, and his absence is immediately noticed by the scavs playing the game, triggering a silent alarm.

Once Rodger has been removed, or the party decides to leave him in the pod (for the moment), proceed to the next beat.

This beat begins once Rodger has been safely removed from the premises, and has 3 potential outcomes + an optional extra bit of summary for any other rescued sleepers, as follows:

If The Other Sleepers Were Rescued

Use when: the other sleepers were awoken at Shady Shallows, and at least some were escorted safely from the premises.

The worst part of the trip back is the smell. Old-person-le-pew, intensified from weeks of not properly bathing. Despite this, though, the old folks are in good spirits, and seem to have fared better in the game than Rodger. Maybe it's from less time spent inside the pods, or maybe they never dove as deep as Rodger did. In either case, they can tell stories of the demon of Acheron, who remained at the top of the leaderboard for their entire, limited stay at Shady Shallows. They are also, of course, quite grateful that you chose to save them.

  • Upon being returned to the Afterlife, David Valdez will take responsibility for the awakened sleepers, finding them rooms in the Afterlife or the surrounding area for a few days, and reconnecting some with their families.

    • If inquired after, Sammy will mention weeks later that his father was fundamentally disturbed by the nightmare fate that can await the elderly in Night City, and has equipped those former sleepers so-willing with considerable resources, forming a private task force to hunt institutions similar to Shady Shallows.

      • Stout will also have tertiary involvement, though after this attempt on his life, the head of house has decided that the Afterlife will make for a suitable retirement spot.

      • If they're ever encountered again, former sleepers will likely recognize their saviors on sight, and use RatSec Elite statblocks.

Critical Condition

Use when: Rodger was removed from the premises but was severely injured in the process and/or the chipware in his head was not removed before leaving.

The trip back to the Afterlife is tense. Stout is in-and-out of consciousness the whole way - periodically he'll wake up and babble about rivers of fire, before seeming to come to his senses - except each time, he insists that he only dozed off while you were with him on an errand run, and keeps calling everyone Thomas, Sam, or Martha, no matter how many times he's corrected. Often, he'll pass out again after only one or two sentences.

Upon returning to the club, Sammy's father is relieved to see Stout - but shocked at his condition. Stout doesn't seem to recognize him any more than anyone else, and David is quick to order the hotel staff to usher his old friend off for medical care.

  • In this ending, the party is provided the base payout by a distracted David and Sammy, but it will be 4d6 in-game days (average of 2 weeks) before Rodger is fit to begin work at the Afterlife.

    • Once Rodger has recovered, Stout has no memory of his rescuers, but retains his memories of Acheron. Once he begins work, the players unlock the Afterlife Hotel services - @UUID[JournalEntry.lFqb5sng3SXQyjfJ]{see the associated journal entry for more information.}

Stable

Use when: Rodger was removed from Shady Shallows safely and without serious harm, and the chipware was found and removed from his socket.

Rodger sleeps soundly most of the way back to the Afterlife. In a couple sporadic bouts of consciousness, he doesn't seem lucid, and he doesn't recognize any of you. He mostly mutters to himself, and falls back asleep quickly.

Upon return, David is relieved to find Rodger in reasonably stable condition, and grateful to the party for his rescue. He arranges for Stout to be cared for within the Afterlife while he recovers from his ordeal, and promises that he'll be paying for a live-in nurse and therapist to assist with Rodger's recovery while there.

  • In this ending, the party is provided the base payout by a grateful David and Sammy, and Rodger will be able to start working within the next 1d6 days thanks to intensive medical care and therapy.

    • Rodger will not remember the party rescuing him, and his memories of Acheron will be clouded. Once he begins work, the players unlock the Afterlife Hotel services - @UUID[JournalEntry.lFqb5sng3SXQyjfJ]{see the associated journal entry for more information.}

In Good Health

Use when: Rodger was removed from Shady Shallows without serious harm, and an anti-Alzheimer's chip was found and inserted into his chipware socket after removing the prior chipware.

In a turn of events that's shocking in its abruptness, Rodger regains consciousness on the way back to the Afterlife. He refuses to answer any questions about how he got to the state he was in, and instead questions you rigorously on where he is, and why he was rescued. He nearly seems more preoccupied with ensuring he properly repays his debt to the individual/organization responsible for organizing his release, than he is about his own condition.

By the time you're back at the Afterlife, he greets Sammy's father formally, but with a friendly air, and insists that David introduce him to the Afterlife's owner immediately so he can attend to his debt for his rescue. David insists that Rodger at least eat something, first, and is quite pleased that your main goal was such a resounding success.

  • In this ending, the party is provided the base payout and, assuming they haven't heavily offended Rodger somehow, he will also authorize their bonus. He remembers most of the rescue and the party, and is quite grateful to be released.

    • He will be able to begin work immediately, and will insist upon doing so as repayment for his rescue. Once he begins work, the players unlock the Afterlife Hotel services - @UUID[JournalEntry.lFqb5sng3SXQyjfJ]{see the associated journal entry for more information.}

Your Feedback Wanted!

Did you enjoy this session? Did something go wrong? Was there broken content? I would very much like to hear about your successes and failures both.

If you have a few minutes after your session, please take the time to fill out the feedback survey for this campaign, located here. It should only take 5 minutes, and will be actively considered as I make revisions to the campaign.

Your feedback matters! Thank you for playing!

The remainder of this mission makes use of the @UUID[Scene.kCCmgpQ8ydS06ngn]{Shady Shallows Retirement Home} scene, along with an environment. To begin:

  • Place @UUID[Actor.hCh5l7uiIPAzHacV]{Aleesha™} at the front desk in the reception area.

  • Place @UUID[Actor.gJAU7fJXKc1lvN2o]{Rodger Stout} in one of the suspension pods (GM discretion). He has 15 health remaining.

    • If mortally wounded, he begins making death saves.

  • Place the player characters in the reception area of the retirement home.

  • Place @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.ZZKdjSmBDqF0RJOT]{RatSec Security Cameras} as follows:

    • 1 overlooks the northwest corner of the main hall, and another watches the southeast.

    • 1 is in the corner of each stasis room, and in the rec room.

    • 1 overlooks the reception area from above its interior doors.

  • Place @UUID[Actor.oYGA773Z7NAtyBkx]{Retirement Home Staff} (actually modified scav savages) as follows:

    • 1 in the office across from Aleesha

    • 1/2*partycount in the administrative room (that's the big one on the bottom of the map)

    • 1/3*partycount more in the rec room (that's the area with the bathroom on the right side)

  • Place a @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.pNTV62sWeONIvn7Z]{Scav Tech} in the chopshop (that's the bloody area on the right side).

  • Off the edge of the map, place the following reinforcements:

    • 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.N1143CZDzlca7sz6]{Scav Butcher} (the building's proprietor and the leader of the gang)

    • 1/2*partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.UM3c8a92eSdEQmB8]{Scav Monsters}

    • 1 @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.pNTV62sWeONIvn7Z]{Scav Tech}

    • 1*partycount @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.SNKnBqS4lCZPd5yU]{Scav Savages.}

    • For every 6 mooks created as reinforcements, create 1 Scav-Tagged @UUID[Compendium.night-city-gang-and-corp-mook-pack.NCGangsnCorpsMooks.Actor.skUkbMc9rOygWl4o]{Generic Van} with a max of 30 HP. Distribute the reinforcements evenly between each van.

Review "The Chop Shop" at the bottom of this entry.

Change the scene to @UUID[Scene.x54RnBX4pYEGzPEl]{New Westbrook Environment.}

Then, read the following.

Shady Shallows Retirement Home

On the edge of New Westbrook, just before the urban hellscape turns to dusty industrial sprawl, you find Shady Shallows. It's a squat, single story concrete building that was probably alright to look at in the 2020s, when it'd been painted and maintained. But it hasn't been, and it shows.

Windows are partially boarded to compensate for broken glass. Scav tagging is everywhere on the surrounding buildings, though it's conspicuously absent on the building you're actually here to see. It looks as though someone unearthed a bunker and skinned its walls for parts.

It isn't active, despite the bustle of New Westbrook around it. No one's going in or out, and evidently the homeless camps that dominate most of the district have decided to steer clear for whatever reason - the parking lot is empty except for a beat up commuter box from the 2020s.

There's a front entrance, and a fire escape on the north side. To the south, there's another entrance - probably administrative. A row of cargo crates sit on the dusty lot out back - maybe containing supplies, maybe more rooms for the retirement home, who knows.

Once inside….

Change the scene to Shady Shallows Retirement Home.

The lobby isn't much to look at - plastic seating is arranged at its edges, and a flickering television screen is alternating between visuals of a fish tank and a UFC rerun every few seconds, like it's gone out of tune somehow. A pair of glass double doors are at the back, showing a hallway that leads north to south.

At the receptionist desk, a bored-looking young woman is doing her makeup, conspicuous for just how out of place she is. Her clothes are designer, her nails immaculate, her hair flawless and her makeup expensive.

Seeing you enter, she snaps her mirror shut and fixes you with an annoyed stare. "Welcome to Shady Shallows; we're closed to visitors for the day, sorry."

The receptionist (Aleesha) is well paid to deflect from the true nature of the retirement home, which she knows nothing about. See her mook sheet for some brief notes on playing her. She's been given very specific instructions:

  • Obstruct anyone who wants to visit their relatives or friends for any reason. A corporate bureaucrat was hired to design a nearly-impenetrable (DV19 Bureaucracy check, reduced to DV17 with 3 or more points) system of checks to stop anyone from getting in.

    • In place of physical visits, AI-driven "live chats" with the guests are enabled, supposedly while they're asleep in the suspension pods at the home. This is a ruse, though it'd be difficult to tell with how addled old folks might be once they're put under, normally.

  • Anyone who comes in to pester the staff - salespeople, procurement, etc. - are to be taken to the back, alone, where they can be "dealt with."

    • To keep Aleesha ignorant, this involves taking them to admin, where they can then escorted by security to the chop shop and disposed of. The scavs aren't fans of anyone, but solicitors especially.

  • "Troublemakers" (rival gangs and edgerunners) are to be turned away - failing that, security is to be notified immediately so they can be "forcibly removed" or otherwise dealt with.

She might flirt with an especially attractive party member, but otherwise, will stubbornly keep the door locked and follow her instructions to the letter; this can only be circumvented by making a DV21 Interrogation (intimidation) or Persuasion check. Otherwise, persuasion attempts will have her show faux-sympathy and offer to schedule a future appointment once the proper forms have been filled in.

She won't fight if attacked, though the scavs will attempt to rescue her.

Once players have made it into the building proper (one way or another), proceed to the next beat.

The Chop Shop

Surprising no one who's been in Night City long enough, a "cheap, affordable, mentally spacious retirement home in New Westbrook" is too good to be true. Rates are cheap, and a pod always seems to be available for that desperate family who need to pawn off gramps once he finally loses his last batch of marbles.

Behind the concrete walls, though, a sinister scene lurks.

  • The medical cyberware that's used by the elderly - synthetic organs, limbs for the frail, anti-Alzheimer's neuralware, etc. - is quite valuable, and desperate families rarely think twice about where it came from.

    • Sure, it wouldn't be that expensive new, but the type of people who buy from scav dealers aren't the type that can afford new to begin with.

  • The prospect being offered holds limited appeal, though - full-dive BD pods manage food and waste, letting the inhabitants live out their personal heavens in their twilight years, restored to bodies lost to time. Popular scenes include:

    • Judgement-free sexual escapades

    • Recreations of old memories

    • Globe-trotting adventures

    • Recreations of action movies with them as the star, or even just being able to spend time with an AI-

  • Despite the scav marketers' best efforts, though, the place has earned a rep - nobody who checks in lasts more than a couple months.

    • It's worth noting that this, sadly, isn't especially rare at any retirement home in the Dark Future.

    • Extended full-dive BD usage isn't healthy even if you had all your mental and physical faculties, which these patients don't.

  • Furthering their cruelty, the scavs won't even let their victims rest peacefully. Instead, they engage them in a full-dive battle royale BD called Acheron, which the scavs themselves also engage in as a source of leisure.

    • The contestants are forced to battle across the hellish plains of a simulated afterlife. Victors are granted Valhalla - a temporary reprieve, and the assurance that they won't be chopped up for parts (unless there's a special order, anyway).

    • Those on the bottom rungs are cut up to make room for new arrivals, and fund the business's continued operation.

    • Unknown to any but the scavs, Rodger Stout has topped this leaderboard for the last couple months, ensuring his survival - for the moment.

  • Could Acheron be the scavs' undoing? - Unknown to the scavs, they've inadvertently been training some truly hardened warriors. The sleepers might not be physically adept in their old age, but then again, the scavs have selected for an excess of cyberware. Wake them up, put a gun in their hands, and you can expect them to be a force to be reckoned with.
